title: High-Protein Chicken Ramen
tagline: Instant ramen turned into a proper gym meal with a seared chicken breast and mushrooms.
servingsNote: 500 g chicken split across two bowls.
macroNotes: Estimated using ~480 kcal instant ramen packets — check your packet, brands vary a lot.
~69 g protein per bowl is excellent for a cut. Most calories are from the fried instant noodles, not the chicken.
To cut ~150-200 kcal: use a baked/air-dried ramen cake instead of fried, skip the oil (use a non-stick pan), and pile in 150-200 g shredded cabbage for volume.
Want more protein? Crack 2 eggs into the simmering broth (egg-drop style) for +12 g — just nudges you slightly over 800 kcal.

items: 250 g raw chicken breast, sliced into thin strips
1 packet instant ramen (~480 kcal) + its seasoning packet
1/2 tsp oil (optional — or use a non-stick pan)

steps.title: Season the chicken
steps.body: Slice 250 g chicken breast into thin strips (thin strips season and cook better than a whole breast). Toss with 1/2 of the ramen seasoning packet, black pepper and a pinch of chilli flakes. Let it sit while you prep everything else.

steps.title: Sear the chicken
steps.body: Get a pan quite hot, add 1/2 tsp oil (or use non-stick dry). Lay the chicken in a single layer and cook 3 minutes on the first side without moving it, then 2-3 minutes on the second side. Let it brown — that's where the flavour is. Remove and set aside.

steps.title: Brown the mushrooms
steps.body: In the same pan, add 100 g sliced mushrooms and cook 3-4 minutes until they release their water and start to brown.

steps.title: Cook the noodles
steps.body: Bring about 400-450 ml water to a boil. Add the noodle cake and cook per packet (usually 3 minutes), then stir in the remaining 1/2 seasoning packet.

steps.title: Bring it together
steps.body: Add the mushrooms and seared chicken back into the pot. Simmer 30-60 seconds, then turn off the heat.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Top with 10 g sliced green onions and extra chilli flakes if you like heat. Serve immediately.

notes: Best-texture trick: use 50-100 ml LESS water than the packet recommends. The broth becomes richer and coats the noodles like a saucy stir-fry instead of a thin, diluted soup.
Cabbage, spinach, ginger, a splash of soy sauce or chilli oil all fit here — cabbage especially adds huge volume for almost no calories.
Cook both bowls at the same time or save half the raw seasoned chicken for the second serving.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
